symptom: Coordinator merge conflicts on shared rebuild files triggered repeated retry loops and burned full retry budgets.
rootCause: Coordinator conflict handling did not fall back to single-agent execution quickly enough, and file ownership inference missed shared targets in subtask descriptions.
addressed: Coordinator now falls back faster and shared-target inference catches more overlap before retries spiral.

symptom: Supervisor subtasks omitted relative shared paths like routes/files.ts, so parallel workers claimed the same file implicitly.
rootCause: Decomposition relied too heavily on explicit files arrays and missed path mentions embedded in freeform task descriptions.
addressed: Relative path extraction added for freeform subtask text, so shared files like routes/files.ts get claimed explicitly.

symptom: Parallel workers still share one physical working tree, so same-file edits can clobber each other before merge logic sees them.
rootCause: Worktree isolation is not in place yet; current mitigation serializes overlapping subtasks instead of isolating each worker filesystem.
addressed: Parallel-safe subtasks now run inside isolated git worktrees before their merged edits are materialized back into the main repo.

symptom: A full 7-step rebuild campaign reached done, but the rebuilt target still failed typecheck, build, and tests during post-run verification.
rootCause: The rebuild runner stopped after per-step success and did not enforce a final integrated typecheck, build, and test gate for the combined target.
addressed: Added final integrated typecheck/build/test gate in rebuild runner, plus one repair pass with captured integration errors.

symptom: 404 This is not a chat model and thus not supported in the v1/chat/completions endpoint.
rootCause: Model resolution accepted stale or unsupported OpenAI-family ids instead of remapping them to supported chat models before hitting Chat Completions.
addressed: Unsupported OpenAI-family ids now normalize to supported chat defaults before Chat Completions requests.

symptom: Verification is inconsistent: it reports Passed: true but also Errors: 24.
rootCause: Review logic previously let the LLM reinterpret successful verification with zero new errors, even when every reported error was pre-existing baseline debt.
addressed: Deterministic review fast-path now treats passed + zero new errors as done, without reinterpreting baseline debt as failure.

symptom: Execution stalled after repeated no-edit rounds while the model kept exploring.
rootCause: The execute watchdog needed stronger nudges and first-edit deadlines to break analysis paralysis on concrete file-target tasks.
addressed: Watchdog strengthened: first-edit deadlines, stronger nudges, repeated identical tool-call breaker, per-step edit baselines, broader no-op justification parsing, and guarded auto-close for preserve-only conditional steps after discovery. Latest rebuild runs still show a later required-edit residual, so benchmark closure stays pending.

symptom: Too many errors (44) introduced. Escalating instead of retrying.
rootCause: Review escalation was too aggressive for cascade failures where one root cause inflated the raw error count across many tests.
addressed: Cascade failures now get one narrow re-plan instead of immediate escalation, then offload if the cascade persists.

symptom: Edited files outside planned scope for explicit upload service targets inside the rebuild repo.
rootCause: Scope guard compared edits only against drafted plan files and missed explicit file targets called out in the instruction itself.
addressed: Scope checks now honor explicit instruction targets, not just drafted plan files.

symptom: Execution stalled after no-edit watchdog rounds even though earlier edits had already landed in the same step.
rootCause: Execute no-edit accounting reset against the current execute attempt instead of a step-scoped edit baseline, so retries forgot prior successful edits in the same plan step.
addressed: Execution now persists a per-step edit baseline across retries, so watchdog checks count edits already completed inside the active step.

symptom: Scope guard blocked canonical api/db edits while the active step still referenced legacy api/src/db targets.
rootCause: Candidate-path scope checks treated canonical api/db paths and legacy api/src/db counterparts as unrelated files instead of equivalent deliverable aliases.
addressed: Scope matching now treats canonical db paths and legacy wrapper paths as equivalent, so canonical deliverables are not blocked by legacy step file lists.

symptom: Evidence-rich no-op completions still tripped the watchdog unless the model emitted the literal NO_EDIT_JUSTIFIED marker.
rootCause: Completion parsing recognized only narrow no-op phrasings, so natural-language evidence like "No edits were needed" or qualified variants such as "No schema edits were needed" still counted as empty completion.
addressed: No-op completion parsing now accepts evidence-rich phrases such as "No edits needed", "No edits were needed", and qualified variants like "No schema edits were needed" when the message includes concrete supporting evidence.

symptom: Later rebuild steps still can end in watchdog offload when the model narrates success or emits bare STEP_COMPLETE after no successful edit landed in a required-edit step.
rootCause: Conditional preserve-only detection missed plain "as needed" compatibility steps, and discovery-before-first-edit tracking was run-global instead of step-local, so later no-op preserve steps still exhausted the watchdog and escalated with bare STEP_COMPLETE.
addressed: Preserve-only compatibility steps now recognize plain "as needed" phrasing, and execute watchdog discovery tracking is step-local instead of run-global, so later no-op preserve steps close cleanly instead of stalling on bare STEP_COMPLETE.

symptom: Required-edit auth hub steps can still stall after an edit_file attempt fails with "old_string matched multiple places" and the model never narrows the replacement context.
rootCause: Deterministic blocker guidance tells the model to re-read and provide more unique edit context, but execute auto-recovery still relies on the model to recover manually from repeated multi-match edit failures in large hub files.
addressed: Not fixed yet. Latest stable-server auth swarm no longer dies on bare STEP_COMPLETE drift; it now advances to a later required-edit failure class: repeated ambiguous multi-match edit context in auth.ts.

symptom: Retryable watchdog stalls escalated out of review instead of retrying execution, so runs stopped early on recoverable no-edit and ambiguous-edit blockers.
rootCause: Review treated all watchdog-class execution issues as terminal offload candidates, even when the blocker was explicitly retryable and execute had deterministic recovery guidance.
addressed: Review now keeps retryable watchdog blockers in the execution loop instead of offloading. Persisted rebuild run 7a2558d0-f8ca-40a7-afd7-b571e5a7d587 shows '[Review] retry (executing)' on watchdog stalls, then continues into verification; later failure is unrelated auth-route repair debt, not the old premature-stop chain.
